extraction.one_line_summary: GILBERT Philippe attacked inside final 6km on late climb, held off KWIATKOWSKI who opened big gap at 300m but faded in final meters
tactical_patterns: KWIATKOWSKI opened decisive gap at 300m marker but tactical timing error—went too early and cracked before line.
WELLENS attacked solo at 32km out in home region, forcing selection but likely wasting energy too early.
GILBERT followed attacks defensively until final 6km climb, then countered when KWIATKOWSKI and others looked around.
HAAS and ALBASINI missed critical wheel in decisive move, showing importance of positioning on penultimate climb.

rider_signals.KWIATKOWSKI Michal: negative: sailed past rivals at 60% effort on climb but premature sprint at 300m revealed poor final-kilometer pacing judgment
rider_signals.GILBERT Philippe: positive: conserved energy following wheels, then perfectly timed attack on final climb to hold off faster finisher
rider_signals.WELLENS Tim: neutral: aggressive 32km solo move in home region showed strength but tactically isolated himself from decisive selection
rider_signals.CERNY Roman: negative: crashed late and Gazprom team awareness was poor—head-in-hands reaction suggests lack of recovery support
rider_signals.HAAS Nathan: negative: strong enough to be in winning move but missed critical wheel when group looked around
